reasoning: Historical 1-week stock moves for broad market equities typically have a 52-54% chance of being positive. CNP's price declined from $44.14 on March 16 down to $41.76 on March 23, but has since rebounded to $42.38 with four consecutive days of upward momentum. The linear model predicts P(>42.38) = 0.55 based on its 60-day trend and standard deviation, though the combined estimate was pulled down to 0.44 purely due to a negative seasonal comparison point from 2025 (P=0.33). Because the past week's momentum is positive, general single-stock one-week drift is slightly positive, and there are no expected non-linear events (earnings/ex-dividend) falling within this early April window, I anchor my probability slightly above a coin toss at 0.51.
